Free Text: Limit of 3 tonnes gross vehicle and plant weight limit applicable to all concourse and platform areas. Requests for dispensation up to a higher limit must be accompanied by a structural assessment and submitted to the Senior Asset Manager for consideration.

ELR Name: EAST COAST NORTH (EDINBURGH WAVERLEY STATION LIMITS )
Free Text: The through lines and some platform lines within the station are electrified by overhead wires and associated equipment and energised at 25 KV

ELR Name: EAST COAST NORTH (EDINBURGH WAVERLEY STATION LIMITS )
Free Text: Requirement to disturb the surface within the station's controlled area, the Buried Services Procedure shall be followed at all times. Work will not be allowed to commence until such time that a Permit to Dig/Penetrate form has been duly signed by the Contract Administrator. It is suspected that low voltage cables are located underground throughout the station. Each contractor will be responsible for the scanning of the work site in order to locate the exact location of the underground power cable

ELR Name: EAST COAST NORTH (EDINBURGH WAVERLEY STATION LIMITS )
Free Text: In addition to the above cabling, Edinburgh Waverley Station contains a large quantity of operational equipment including Signalling and Telecommunications <S&T> cables and train dispatch equipment. Any contractor proposing to undertake any demolition, excavation or works that could disturb any cables should refer to the National Hazard Directory.

ELR Name: EAST COAST NORTH (EDINBURGH WAVERLEY STATION LIMITS )
Free Text: Records of the drainage layout although limited are indicative of main runs and are held by the Senior Asset Manager and the Facility Delivery Manager. Contractors should note that drainage exists beneath platforms.

ELR Name: EAST COAST NORTH (EDINBURGH WAVERLEY STATION LIMITS )
Free Text: Records of the water supply pipeline layouts although limited are indicative of main runs and are held by the Senior Asset Manager and the Facility Delivery Manager. Contractors should note that drainage exists beneath platforms.

ELR Name: EAST COAST NORTH (EDINBURGH WAVERLEY STATION LIMITS )
Free Text: Records of gas supply pipelines are held by the Senior Asset Manager and the Facility Delivery Manager. All mains gas supply pipes up to and including the meter are the responsibility of National Grid. In case of emergency contact 0800 111 999. Natural gas is non-toxic, but large concentrations could cause suffocation. It is highly flammable and leakage from a pipeline could result in an explosive mixture with air. Noise of escaping gas could be sufficient to cause damage to hearing and ear protection must be worn. All persons must be kept 100 metres from the damaged pipeline.

ELR Name: EAST COAST NORTH (EDINBURGH WAVERLEY STATION LIMITS )
Free Text: Asbestos hazards please see ARMS for specific details

ELR Name: EAST COAST NORTH (EDINBURGH WAVERLEY STATION LIMITS )
Free Text: Within and in the voids beneath the platforms there is the likelihood of the presence of vermin. This is particularly the case in those arches, which are currently unused and contain large amounts of rubbish, thus providing a potential breeding ground. Contractors should be aware that poison has been put down at various locations.

ELR Name: EAST COAST NORTH (EDINBURGH WAVERLEY STATION LIMITS )
Free Text: Many panes of glass are missing from the canopy roof and special precautions apply regarding contact with electrical equipment in the presence of water.

ELR Name: EAST COAST NORTH (EDINBURGH WAVERLEY STATION LIMITS )
Free Text: The existing train shed is in a poor state of repair with corrosion to many of the steel and cast members which in turn support timber trusses in various stages of deterioration and rot, so caution should be taken when working on the roof. The roof is coming up for a complete refurbishment in 2010 - 2014. Further safety information about working on the roof can be found in Edinburgh Waverley Station's "Station Safety Briefing" C-06 and C-38. Three areas of the roof have been refurbished and covered with ETFE fabric membrane, which has all been delineation with warning signs between the covered and non-covered parts of the roof. To allow the new covered roof area to be maintained and to be inspected regularly, a safe form of access and maintenance strategy has been devised.

ELR Name: EAST COAST NORTH (EDINBURGH WAVERLEY STATION LIMITS )
Free Text: Roof above NXEC First Class Lounge. Heating and ventilation system equipment is contained in this area. Wind blown litter and that thrown from the North Bridge above, may accumulate very quickly. There are different floor levels, including access from raised threshold at the entrance door, slabs and bases of fall protection.

ELR Name: EAST COAST NORTH (EDINBURGH WAVERLEY STATION LIMITS )
Free Text: Basement Room 2, off Bridge Pier Corridor - Boiler room. The room contains boiler, pump and associated pipework and controls.

ELR Name: EAST COAST NORTH (EDINBURGH WAVERLEY STATION LIMITS )
Free Text: Basement Room 1, off Bridge Pier Corridor - Boiler House Ventilation Plant room. The room contains equipment and pipework. The floor within this room is uneven. Below the lift there is a void, which should be considered as limited access.

ELR Name: EAST COAST NORTH (EDINBURGH WAVERLEY STATION LIMITS )
Free Text: Platform 11, back of Scotrail building <south ramp> - Boiler room. This room houses two gas boilers and associated controls and pipework, much of which is overhead and around walls. The main hazards associated with this area are electrical, water, fire and explosion.

ELR Name: EAST COAST NORTH (EDINBURGH WAVERLEY STATION LIMITS )
Free Text: Sub-basement, off Bridge Pier Corridor. Houses large pipework giving rise to limited headroom <5? approx>. This area should be considered as Limited Access/ Confined Space and suitable controls applied.

ELR Name: EAST COAST NORTH (EDINBURGH WAVERLEY STATION LIMITS )
Free Text: Basement - HV Substation A <main>. Only authorised persons are permitted entry. The hazards associated with these areas are mainly electrical.

ELR Name: EAST COAST NORTH (EDINBURGH WAVERLEY STATION LIMITS )
Free Text: Signalling Centre - Scottish Power HV Substation B. This sub station is owned and controlled by Scottish Power. Only authorised persons are permitted entry. Please note this is not Sub Station `B? as this is located at the signalling centre and does not form part of this directory.

ELR Name: EAST COAST NORTH (EDINBURGH WAVERLEY STATION LIMITS )
Free Text: Platform 3/ back of ScotRail operations depot - HV Substations C. This caged area has an uneven floor and the metal floor ducts appear to be 2" approx. deep. Only authorised persons are permitted entry. The main hazards are electrical, tripping and wind blown rubbish creating a fire hazard.

ELR Name: EAST COAST NORTH (EDINBURGH WAVERLEY STATION LIMITS )
Free Text: SCOTTISH POWER HIGH VOLTAGE SUB STATION AND METERS ARE LOCATED TRACKSIDE OF PLATFORM 9. ACCESS TO THE SUB STATION IS OBTAINED FROM THE OVER BRIDGE AND A PURPOSE BUILT LADDER AND WALKWAY. THE KEYS FROM THE OVER BRIDGE ARE HELD BY THE STATION MANAGEMENT. THE WALKWAY FORMS A PROTECTION SSOW FROM THE INFRASTRUCTURE AND POSSESSIONS ARE NOT REQUIRED.

ELR Name: EAST COAST NORTH (EDINBURGH WAVERLEY STATION LIMITS )
Free Text: Basement Room 8, off Bridge Pier Corridor - LV switch room <main>. This room contains numerous cables and pipework. In addition there is a metal floor duct for cables. The main hazard associated with this room is electrical.

ELR Name: EAST COAST NORTH (EDINBURGH WAVERLEY STATION LIMITS )
Free Text: North Block Building, first floor - Plant room. There are numerous pipes and cables, and a raised floor level present.

ELR Name: EAST COAST NORTH (EDINBURGH WAVERLEY STATION LIMITS )
Free Text: South Block Building - Plant room. The access to the Plant Room has various floor levels, raised threshold at entrance door and other raised levels. There are numerous pipes and cables with low headroom in areas. Hazards associated with this area are confined space / limited access, trips, head bumps and electrical.

ELR Name: EAST COAST NORTH (EDINBURGH WAVERLEY STATION LIMITS )
Free Text: This room contains heating and ventilation equipment. There are discarded machine parts and a switchboard with loose cables exposing wires.

ELR Name: EAST COAST NORTH (EDINBURGH WAVERLEY STATION LIMITS )
Free Text: This room contains machinery for the Balmoral Hotel goods lift. The entrance is 1½? x 3? approx. and inside there is no floor but the cables and workings for the lift. This should be considered limited access. Hazards associated with this area are electrical, mechanical and falls; also those contained in limited access situations.

ELR Name: EAST COAST NORTH (EDINBURGH WAVERLEY STATION LIMITS )
Free Text: Includes pipework, valves and a raised platform.

ELR Name: EAST COAST NORTH (EDINBURGH WAVERLEY STATION LIMITS )
Free Text: The entrance has a raised threshold and there are other raised levels. There are cables, ducts and pipes present within the room associated with the plant.

ELR Name: EAST COAST NORTH (EDINBURGH WAVERLEY STATION LIMITS )
Free Text: This room has been used for pistol shooting practice, a charity bicycles store but at the moment it is empty. There are pipes and cables present with high voltage cable of 11 kV present in the room. In addition there are 440v sub mains cables. Within this area there is also a main intermediate pressure gas pipe and mains pressure water pipe..

ELR Name: EAST COAST NORTH (EDINBURGH WAVERLEY STATION LIMITS )
Free Text: Basement Room 6, off Bridge Pier Corridor - Calorifer. This room contains numerous lagged pipework and electrical controls associated with the equipment. It is believed that the tanks are lagged with asbestos material and it has been registered.

ELR Name: EAST COAST NORTH (EDINBURGH WAVERLEY STATION LIMITS )
Free Text: Arches below New Street Car Park - The Vaults. The vaults lie directly below much of the car park and is a series of arches that is entered from a metal gate in New Street. Rooms off the first arch have wood strewn around, there are loading bays exposing risks of falls, various items of discarded materials. There are some manholes within the main central passage, which could not be removed and therefore be considered as confined spaces/limited access. Much of the electrical equipment has been water damaged and out of use, but some is live since the lighting tends to be on constantly.

ELR Name: EAST COAST NORTH (EDINBURGH WAVERLEY STATION LIMITS )
Free Text: Service Duct below concourse to the east, north and south of station building - entered by manhole opposite taste unit, manhole at south east concourse. Classified as Confined Space. ELR Name: EDINBURGH & GLASGOW MAIN (WAVERLEY STN LIMITS - COM HAYMARKET STATION)
Free Text: Further to recent incidents of rock-fall within the locale of the PSG access via Kings Stables Road we must insist on the following procedures being adopted when using this authorised access point: All Network Rail and Edinburgh City Council gates MUST be locked behind you on entry/exit. Only once the possession/blockage is granted should all staff/equipment be taken to site; then the vehicle MUST be parked away from the PSG access point. Historic Scotland have gracefully requested us to observe the above instructions to ensure that no damage is sustained to this conservation area, particularly the SSSI site at Princes St Gardens or the listed St Margaret's Well at this World Heritage Site. A new bat roost has been installed near the Reston Station RRAP. Initially bats were found to be roosting in a lineside building near the RRAP, but the building required partial demolition, affecting the roosting location. An ecology report concluded that a replacement roost could be placed nearby which would have minimal disturbance to the roosting behaviours. This was placed across the track near an area of shrubbery, and was pre-made. Note to be made that the roost is near shrubbery, so in case of any vegetaion works, special care to be taken to ensure that machinery should not interfere with the roosting habits of the bats <ensure not to knock/bumb etc>
